---
title: "Characteristics of Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo Penguins"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project"
author: "Michelle Chow"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.7"
  data-slide-number: 1
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
  
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{r}
#| label: load-packages
#| include: false

# Load packages here
pacman::p_load(tidymodels,
               tidyverse)

```

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

# Plot theme
ggplot2::theme_set(ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 11))

# For better figure resolution
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.retina = 3, 
  dpi = 300, 
  fig.width = 6, 
  fig.asp = 0.618 
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: load-data
#| include: false
# Load data here
penguin <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-04-15/penguins.csv')
```

## Motivation

- Fascination for penguins

- Global warming impacting penguin colonies

  - Essential to track characteristics of penguins that can impact colony health

## Data

::: row
```{r}
penguin |> 
  head() |>
  kableExtra::kable() |>
  kableExtra::kable_styling(font_size = 16, full_width = FALSE) |>
  kableExtra::scroll_box(width = "100%")
```
:::

::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
:::{.incremental}

- Palmer Penguins dataset

- First created in TidyTuesday in July 2020

  - Updated in 2025

- Contains 8 columns and 344 rows of data

  - 3 species penguins

  - 8 different penguin characteristics

  - 3 islands

- Focus on island, species, year and body mass variables
:::
:::

## Questions
:::{.incremental}
1.  How does the island where Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap penguins reside impact their bill length?
2.  How has the mean body mass of the Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap penguins changed between 2007 and 2009 and does the island impact the mass?
:::

# Visualizations
## Visualizations - Question 1
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Create a bar plot showing which islands the Gentoo, Adelie, and Chinstrap penguins inhabit
ggplot(penguin, aes(x = island, fill = species)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    x = "Island",
    y = "No. of Penguins",
    fill = "Penguin Species",
    title = "Number of Penguins on Biscoe, Dream, and Torgersen"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em; text-align:center;"}
:::{.incremental}
- Only Adelie penguins inhabit all islands
- Gentoo penguins have the largest penguin population on Biscoe
- Chinstrap penguins live on Dream
- Adelie penguins had similar number of observations as Gentoo penguins (\~120)
:::
:::
:::
:::::

## Visualizations - Question 1
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Create a box plot of Gentoo, Adelie, and Chinstrap penguin bill lengths
ggplot(penguin, aes(x = species, y = bill_len, fill = island)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    x = "Penguin Species",
    y = "Bill length (mm)",
    title = "Bill length of Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo Penguins",
    fill = "Island"
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 22, size = 5, color = NA))) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em; text-align:center;"}
:::{.incremental}
- Chinstrap had the longest median bill length
- Adelie had the shortest median bill length
- Gentoo outlier with a bill length of 60mm
- Similar Adelie penguin bill lengths across 3 islands
  - Biscoe had the least variation
- Chinstrap has the largest variation in bill length compared to all species
:::
:::
:::
:::::

## Visualizations - Question 1
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Create a violin plot of the distribution of bill lengths for each of the penguin species
ggplot(penguin, aes(x = species, y = bill_len, fill = island)) +
  geom_violin() +
  labs(
    x = "Penguin Species",
    y = "Bill length (mm)",
    fill = "Island",
    title = "Bill length of Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo Penguins"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
:::{.incremental}
All species:

- Most chinstrap penguins have a bill length of 51mm
- Moderate amount of Chinstrap penguins with a bill length of 41mm
- Gentoo penguins have a bill length of 45mm to 50mm
- Right-skewed
- Adelie penguins have a bill length of 37mm to 41mm
:::
:::
:::
::: {.column width="25%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em;"}
:::{.incremental}
Specific to Adelie penguins:

- Biscoe and Torgersen penguins tend to have longer bill lengths

- Distribution of Torgersen bill lengths more symmetrical

- Biscoe penguins are right-skewed

- Dream penguins are left-skewed
:::
:::
:::
:::::

## Visualizations - Question 2
::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Calculate the mean body mass of the Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap penguin for 2007, 2008, and 2009
penguin_mean <- penguin |>
  group_by(year, species) |>
  summarize(
    mean_body_mass = mean(body_mass, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
# Create a time series plot to compare the mean body mass of Adelie, Gentoo, and Chinstrap penguins between 2007 and 2009
ggplot(
  penguin_mean, 
  aes(x = factor(year), y = mean_body_mass, color = species, group = species)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    labs(
      x = "Year",
      y = "Mean Body Mass (g)",
      color = "Species",
      title = "Mean Body Mass of Adelie, Chinstrap, and Gentoo penguins between 2007 and 2009"
    ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5))
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em; text-align:center;"}
:::{.incremental}
- Gentoo penguins have the highest mean body mass (\~5100g)
- Adelie and Chinstrap penguins have lower mean body mass (\~3700g)
- +50g net change to Gentoo penguins mean body mass
- -50g net change to Adelie penguins mean body mass
- Chinstrap penguins mean body mass remains unchanged
:::
:::
:::
:::::

## Visualizations - Question 2

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Calculate the Adelie penguins' mean body mass between 2007 and 2009 for each of the 3 islands
adelie_mean <- penguin |>
  filter(species == "Adelie") |>
  group_by(year, island) |>
  summarize(
    mean_body_mass = mean(body_mass, na.rm = TRUE),
  )
# Create a time series plot to compare the mean body mass of Adelie penguins between 2007 and 2009 for each of the 3 islands
ggplot(
  adelie_mean,
  aes(x = factor(year),
  y = mean_body_mass,
  color = island,
  group = island
  )
) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Body Mass of Adelie penguins between 2007 and 2009",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Mean Body Mass (g)",
    color = "Island"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5))
```
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em; text-align:center;"}
:::{.incremental}
- Torgersen Adelie penguins mean body mass decreased drastically (\~250g drop)
- May be cause for concern
- Biscoe Adelie penguins mean body mass increased drastically (\~250g increase)
- Dream Adelie penguins remains mostly unchanged
:::
:::
:::
:::::

# Conclusion

## Conclusion - Question 1
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}

*All species*

- Chinstrap penguins correlated with longer bills but have more variation in bill length

- Adelie penguins correlated with shorter bills and have the least variation in bill length

*Adelie penguins*

- Most symmetrical distribution for Torgersen Adelie penguins

- Dream Adelie penguins correlated with shorter bill lengths than the other 2 islands
:::

## Conclusion - Question 2
::: {style="font-size: 0.5em"}
*All species*

- Gentoo penguins have the highest mean body mass (\~5100g)

- Adelie and Chinstrap penguins had similar mean body mass (\~3700g)

- Gentoo penguin had net positive change in mean body mass

- Adelie penguin had net negative change in mean body mass

- Chinstrap penguin mean body mass remains unchanged

*Adelie penguins*

- Biscoe penguins net increase in mean body mass between 2007 and 2009 (3625g to 3850g)

- Dream penguins net decrease in mean body mass between 2007 and 2009 (3675g to 3650g)

- Torgersen penguins net decrease in mean body mass between 2007 and 2009 (3755g to less than 3500g)
:::

## Images

![Image credit: Danielle Navarro, Percolate.](images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.png){fig-align="center" width="500"}
